stimulus.content: Burning fossil fuels like coal, oil, and natural gas releases gases into the atmosphere. These gases trap heat from the sun, causing Earth's average temperature to rise slowly over time. Scientists call this process global warming.

stem: According to the passage, what is the main cause of global warming?

choices.text: Gases from burning fossil fuels trap heat in the air
The sun is getting closer to Earth every year
Forests are growing too quickly around the world
Oceans are releasing cold water into the atmosphere

answer_explanation: The passage explains that burning fossil fuels releases greenhouse gases that trap heat in the atmosphere, causing Earth's temperature to rise over time.
